describe: DL-22 quotas LIVE-WIRED at the forge-core command/host boundary: a real over-quota ctx.db write (runtime.run) is REJECTED with data byte-intact (never deleted); an approaching-limit write is ACCEPTED but surfaces a non-blocking warning (event + response field) suggesting compaction/cleanup/export; attachments dedup by content hash through the workspace seam; quota.status reports usage vs. trusted limits deterministically; quota.set is Owner-only trusted config (not the request payload). Driven by forge-core/tests/quota_core_conformance.rs through WorkspaceCore::handle / put_attachment.

assert_kinds.db_write: drive runtime.run (a real ctx.db.insert): seed N runs, apply the trusted quota.set, run again; assert the run is accepted (optionally with an approaching warning + suggestion) or rejected (typed ResourceLimitExceeded + suggestion), and on rejection prove prior records + quota.status usage are byte-intact and the rejected record never landed
assert_kinds.attachment: put_attachment the listed byte strings through the workspace seam; assert per-put stored_new/refcount, that identical bytes dedup to one stored blob, and that quota.status attachments bytes are unchanged across a dedup hit
assert_kinds.status: seed records via runtime.run, then quota.status: assert the usage shape (applets/categories/workspace total), the effective policy, the approaching list, and that two reads are byte-equal (deterministic)
assert_kinds.set: quota.set is Owner-only trusted config: a non-owner role is PermissionDenied; an Owner set is persisted and read back by quota.status; the policy is the trusted state, not the request payload
